name: 1950s & 1960s
description: The golden age of die-cast. Lesney, Dinky, and early Corgi pieces that started it all.

name: 1970s
description: Hot Wheels Redlines, Matchbox Superfast, and muscle-car mania in miniature.

name: 1980s
description: Neon, cassette decks, and a wave of European diecast from Majorette and Siku.

name: 1990s
description: Treasure Hunts, Real Riders, and the collector's-series boom.

name: 2000s & Newer
description: Premium lines, licensed replicas, and limited-edition modern releases.

name: Hot Wheels
description: The world's best-selling toy car brand. Redlines, Flying Colors, Treasure Hunts, and beyond.

name: Matchbox
description: Lesney originals and Superfast era pieces — British heritage at 1:64.

name: Corgi
description: Renowned for detailed interiors, opening features, and iconic movie tie-ins.

name: Johnny Lightning
description: Topper Toys originals and the 1990s revival collector lines.

name: Dinky Toys
description: Meccano's legendary British die-casts, sought after by serious collectors worldwide.

name: Majorette
description: French flair in miniature — excellent detail and robust construction.

name: 1:64 Scale
description: The classic pocket-size format — the standard for Hot Wheels and Matchbox.

name: 1:43 Scale
description: Mid-size prestige format favoured by Corgi and Dinky for incredible detail.

name: 1:18 Scale
description: Display-grade replicas with opening hoods, doors, and fine interior detail.

name: Muscle Cars
description: Mustangs, Camaros, Challengers, Chargers — American horsepower frozen in metal.

name: Sports Cars
description: Ferraris, Porsches, Aston Martins — European precision at collectible scale.

name: Movie & TV Cars
description: Batmobiles, Bond cars, and screen icons immortalised as die-cast.

name: Mint in Box
description: Unplayed-with, original packaging intact. The pinnacle of collector condition.
